memberdef.name: SYN_MAILBOX_DEFINE
memberdef.initializer: static uint8_t name##_buf[(count) * sizeof(type)]; \ static SYN_Mailbox name = { \ .buf = name##_buf, \ .msg_size = sizeof(type), \ .capacity = (count), \ .head = 0, \ .tail = 0, \ .overflow = 0, \ }

detaileddescription.para:
Creates a mailbox instance with embedded storage.
name
Variable name.
type
Message struct type.
count
Max number of messages.

parameterdescription.para: Size of one message in bytes.

detaileddescription.para: Uses STORE_RELEASE on the head update to ensure message data is committed before the consumer can see the new index.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to message data (msg_size bytes copied).

simplesect.para: true if posted, false if full.

detaileddescription.para: Uses LOAD_ACQUIRE on the head read to ensure all message data written by the producer is visible. Uses STORE_RELEASE on the tail update to ensure the producer sees the freed slot.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to receive buffer (msg_size bytes copied out).

simplesect.para: true if a message was received, false if empty.

briefdescription.para: Peek at the next message without consuming it.

simplesect.para: Pointer to the message, or NULL if empty. Valid until the next receive().

briefdescription.para: Check if the mailbox is empty.
Check if the mailbox is full.
Number of messages currently in the mailbox.
Typed message queue — fixed-size SPSC ring for inter-task IPC.

detaileddescription.para: Passes fixed-size messages between producer (ISR or task) and consumer (task). Lock-free single-producer single-consumer design.

When SYN_USE_MULTICORE is enabled, acquire/release memory barriers are inserted at every index access, making the mailbox safe for cross-core communication (core A posts, core B receives). When multicore is disabled (the default), these compile to plain volatile reads and writes — zero overhead.

Correctness model (multicore)
The mailbox has exactly two indices:
head — written ONLY by the producer
tail — written ONLY by the consumer

listitem.para: Read tail (LOAD_ACQUIRE) — observe consumer's latest progress
memcpy message data into buf[head * msg_size]
Write head = next (STORE_RELEASE) — data committed before index
Read head (LOAD_ACQUIRE) — observe producer's latest index
memcpy message from buf[tail * msg_size] — safe: data visible
Write tail = next (STORE_RELEASE) — slot freed for producer
